title: styrax nicaraguensis subsp. nicaraguensis
intro: Styrax nicaraguensis subsp. nicaraguensis, also known as the Nicaraguan styrax, is a species of tree native to Central America, characterized by its fragrant resin. The resin of this plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits in treating wounds and skin conditions, as well as reducing fever and pain. In herbal preparations, the resin of Styrax nicaraguensis subsp. nicaraguensis is often extracted and used in topical creams, ointments, and salves to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the resin has been used in traditional folk medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and is also valued for its antiseptic properties.
